title: A retina chip that restores sight is going on sale in Europe
excerpt: Geographic atrophy is the worst form of age-related macular degeneration. For most people who have it, the centre of the world slowly greys out. Faces blur. Words vanish. There is no cure, only treatments that slow the loss. Now a small chip is giving some of that sight back. Science Corp, based in Alameda, California, […]
content_html:
Geographic atrophy is the worst form of age-related macular degeneration. For most people who have it, the centre of the world slowly greys out. Faces blur. Words vanish. There is no cure, only treatments that slow the loss. Now a small chip is giving some of that sight back.

Science Corp, based in Alameda, California, will begin selling its Prima implant in Europe, the company said. The device is a chip that sits at the back of the eye and works with a special pair of glasses.

The glasses beam near-infrared light to the chip, which converts it into signals for the retina. A “zoom” feature lets patients magnify letters. Prima has earned a CE mark from the notified body DEKRA, Bloomberg reported, clearing it for sale across 30 European countries.

The milestone is not only medical. This is the first device from a US brain-implant company to reach patients widely. The firm that got there is not the famous one. Science is the second most valuable US brain-implant company, behind Elon Musk’s Neuralink. Its founder, Max Hodak, helped start Neuralink before leaving to build this.

“This is the first in hopefully a long series of commercial products that proceed to advance that horizon, sometimes incrementally, but relentlessly,” Hodak said in an interview.

The word that matters there is commercial. Brain-implant companies have run trials for years. Selling a device to ordinary patients is a different threshold.

What the chip can, and cannot, do

The trial behind the approval was small but striking. Published in the New England Journal of Medicine, it followed 38 patients across 17 sites in five countries. On average they gained 25.5 letters, more than five lines on a standard eye chart. And 84% could again read letters, numbers, and words. It is not a cure.

Geographic atrophy from AMD affects more than five million people worldwide. But it is a genuine return of function. Science has raised $489m at a $1.5bn valuation.

Europe first, America later

The geography is telling. Science expects its first commercial patient in August, probably in Germany, then dozens more this year and around 200 next.

The US comes later. The company is working with the Food and Drug Administration and hopes to reach the US market next year. Prima already holds FDA Breakthrough Device and Humanitarian Use Device designations, both meant to speed narrow, high-need treatments.

Meanwhile China has named brain tech a national priority and cleared its own first implant. The global race is drawing in billions. The science that restores sight is arriving. Where you can buy it, and what it will cost, is the next question. For now the answer is simple: Europe, price unset.

sentences.en: Meanwhile China has named brain tech a national priority and cleared its own first implant.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，中国将脑技术列为国家优先事项，并批准了自己的首个植入物。

sentences.en: The global race is drawing in billions.
sentences.zh: 全球竞赛吸引了数十亿美元。

sentences.en: The science that restores sight is arriving.
sentences.zh: 恢复视力的科学即将到来。

sentences.en: Where you can buy it, and what it will cost, is the next question.
sentences.zh: 下一个问题是在哪里可以买到它，以及它的价格是多少。

sentences.en: For now the answer is simple: Europe, price unset.
sentences.zh: 目前答案很简单：欧洲，价格未定。

content_html_zh:
地理性萎缩是年龄相关性黄斑变性的最严重形式。 对于大多数拥有它的人来说，世界中心慢慢变灰。 面孔模糊。 言语消失。 没有治愈方法，只有减缓损失的治疗方法。 现在，一个小芯片正在恢复一些这种景象。

总部位于加利福尼亚州阿拉米达的 Science Corp 表示，该公司将开始在欧洲销售其 Prima 植入物。 该设备是一个位于眼睛后部的芯片，与一副特殊的眼镜配合使用。

眼镜将近红外光发射到芯片上，芯片将其转换成视网膜信号。 “缩放”功能可以让患者放大字母。 据彭博社报道，Prima 已获得认证机构 DEKRA 颁发的 CE 标志，从而可以在 30 个欧洲国家销售。

这一里程碑不仅是医学上的。 这是美国大脑植入公司第一个广泛应用于患者的设备。 到达那里的公司并不是著名的公司。 Science 是美国第二大最有价值的大脑植入公司，仅次于埃隆·马斯克的 Neuralink。 其创始人马克斯·霍达克 (Max Hodak) 在离职前帮助创办了 Neuralink。

霍达克在接受采访时表示：“这是一系列商业产品中的第一个，这些产品将继续推进这一领域，有时是渐进式的，但要坚持不懈。”

那里最重要的词是商业。 大脑植入公司已经进行了多年的试验。 向普通患者销售设备是一个不同的门槛。

芯片能做什么、不能做什么

批准背后的试验虽小但引人注目。 该研究发表在《新英格兰医学杂志》上，对五个国家 17 个地点的 38 名患者进行了随访。 他们平均增加了 25.5 个字母，超过标准视力表上的 5 行。 84% 的人可以再次阅读字母、数字和单词。 这不是治愈方法。

AMD 造成的地理萎缩影响了全球超过 500 万人。 但这是真正的功能回归。 Science 已筹集 4.89 亿美元，估值为 15 亿美元。

先欧洲，后美洲

地理很能说明问题。 《Science》预计 8 月份将迎来第一个商业患者，可能会在德国，今年还会有数十个患者，明年将有大约 200 个患者。

美国来得晚一些。 该公司正在与美国食品和药物管理局合作，希望明年进入美国市场。 Prima 已获得 FDA 突破性设备和人道主义用途设备称号，这两项称号都旨在加速狭窄、高需求的治疗。

与此同时，中国将脑技术列为国家优先事项，并批准了自己的首个植入物。 全球竞赛吸引了数十亿美元。 恢复视力的科学即将到来。 下一个问题是在哪里可以买到它，以及它的价格是多少。 目前答案很简单：欧洲，价格未定。
